live_fields.value: MercadoLibre provides NO forward numeric guidance (long-standing practice). CFO Martin de los Santos on the Q1 2026 call: 'we avoid talking about margins on a quarterly forward-looking basis.' Qualitative Q1'26 outlook: continue to prioritize long-term growth investment over short-term margin ('the right response is not to harvest -- it is to invest'); margin compression expected to persist near-term; credit-card portfolio growing >100% YoY; Brazil free-shipping threshold lowered (R$19) with select take-rate cuts effective Q2 2026 (not in Q1 numbers); logistics cost/shipment -17% YoY. No explicit revenue/EPS/EBITDA targets.

No material new litigation or regulatory action identified as of as_of that is thesis-load-bearing. Standard LatAm tax disputes (esp. Brazil ICMS/PIS-COFINS and Argentine tax matters) are recurring for MELI and disclosed as contingencies, but are ordinary-course and not a new event. Key LIVE risk items are macro/regulatory, not litigation: (a) Brazil/PIX and consumer-credit regulation; (b) Argentine FX/capital-controls and IAS 29 hyperinflation accounting; (c) potential LatAm digital-tax/cross-border-import rule changes (Brazil 'taxa das blusinhas' import taxation of low-value cross-border parcels, aimed at Shein/Temu, indirectly relevant). None constitute a disclosed material loss as of as_of.
